free_text: Absence of the threshold anomaly in the elastic
scattering of the weakly bound projectile 7-Li on 27-Al
Laboratory TANDAR.
Institute of Physics.
A set of eight silicon surface-barrier
detectors was used to measure the elastic scattering
cross sections at different angles.  These angular
distributions were taken in steps of 2-5 degrees
depending on the energy and angular range.  The angular
resolution of each detector was less than 0.5 degree
and their energy resolution ranged from 0.5 to 1%.
The target was a 70 mug/cm2 thick 27-Al foil.
20 UD accelerator.
Typical beam currents were below 50 nA.
See SAMPLE.
An electron suppressed Faraday cup and a
monitor detector at 15 degree.
The estimated overall uncertainty of the
results ranges from 5 to 15%.
Volume integrals.
By F.Ch
Data tables were received from J.M.
Figueira.
New reference was added.
-By authors.  Although no direct charge identification
was performed in this experiment, transfer and
inelastic scattering channels can be easily separated
from the elastic peak since they contribute to
different position in the energy spectra.
